message:
fix(core/abi/fuzz): 4 real library bugs + 1 harness bug from review-mode fuzz sweep

- **bd-ie8zc (stdlib atoi)**: atoi clamped to INT_MAX on overflow
  instead of truncating. POSIX SUSv4: atoi(str) ≡ (int)strtol(str,
  NULL, 10) — a truncation cast. Replace clamp with bare `val as i32`.

- **bd-xx2j1 (stdio_abi %n)**: in hardened mode, %n silently skipped
  the write when the caller's count pointer wasn't in our tracked
  allocator. Stack locals and external mallocs fall here —
  effectively broke %n for every normal caller. Absence of allocator
  tracking is not evidence of danger; only set should_write=false
  when we have positive evidence (rem < size).

- **bd-35vob (scanf scan_char)**: unchecked `pos + n` add where n is
  spec.width — overflows usize on crafted huge width. Debug panics;
  release UB would skip bounds check and read past input. Fix:
  checked_add + early-return None on overflow.

- **bd-reb0e (fuzz_pidfd_timer harness, already committed in 2250242a)**:
  TimerDelete id-reuse pattern same as bd-2t616 PidfdClose.

- **bd-gayb3 (fuzz_scanf harness)**: ScanValue::Char non-empty
  assertion rejected legitimate %0c zero-width result. Relax to
  `let _ = bytes.len()`.
